steps.title: Simplify the given expression
steps.content: Since $y > 0$, $\sqrt{y^8} = y^4$. Substitute and simplify: $\frac{3x^{17}y^5}{5x^2 y^4} = \frac{3x^{15}y}{5}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — I only: Form I is $\frac{x^{14/3}y^{5/3}}{3}$. This has exponent $\frac{14}{3}$ on $x$ and $\frac{5}{3}$ on $y$, and a denominator of 3 — none of these match $\frac{3x^{15}y}{5}$. Form I is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice B — II only: Form II is $\frac{3x^{11}y^2}{5}$. The exponents $x^{11}$ and $y^2$ do not match $x^{15}$ and $y^1$ in the simplified expression. Form II is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice C — I and II: Since neither Form I nor Form II equals $\frac{3x^{15}y}{5}$, both cannot be correct. ✗
  • Choice D — Neither I nor II: Neither form is equivalent to the simplified expression $\frac{3x^{15}y}{5}$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
